candidates.title: Resolve Batch 25 leakage-risk hypotheses under Protocol Cards

sourceEvidence.title: Batch 21 Protocol-Card Replay Week 1
Batch 22 Protocol-Card Replay Model-Family Stress
Batch 24 Protocol-Card Kill Week and Next Frontier
Batch 25 Leakage-Risk Cards Week 1

candidates.whyNow: Batch 25 left group/file overlap, class-imbalance-mimics-leakage, and protocol ambiguity as unresolved hypotheses.
candidates.expectedScientificValue: Narrows whether split deltas are leakage-like mechanisms or ordinary protocol difficulty.
candidates.falsifiability: Leakage claims are rejected unless duplicate/group/file/feature overlap evidence survives controls.
candidates.executionFeasibility: Uses existing Protocol Cards, Leakage-Risk Cards, and public corpus evidence.
expectedNegativeControls: shuffled-label or random-label control where applicable
candidates.safetyScope: safe computational research only; no medical advice, wet-lab protocol, exploit, malware, private data, or legal patentability claim

candidates.title: Repo/test reproduction with runtime evidence for scientist CLI

sourceEvidence.title: RapidFuzz Repository Install and Example Reproduction Audit
MarkupSafe Repository Install and Test Reproduction
itsdangerous Repository Install and Test Reproduction
scikit-learn Iris Example Reproduction Ladder

candidates.whyNow: Prior tool decisions kept pytest_repro_summary support-only; runtime evidence is the next constraint.
candidates.expectedScientificValue: Tests whether the scientist layer can use a different route than leakage-risk detection.
candidates.falsifiability: The route is partial if it cannot execute repository code or produce replay evidence.
candidates.executionFeasibility: Runs local public repo code and CLI probes without private data or network.
candidates.safetyScope: safe computational research only; no medical advice, wet-lab protocol, exploit, malware, private data, or legal patentability claim

candidates.title: Recheck tool usefulness beyond simple baselines

sourceEvidence.title: Batch 10 Toolchain Kill Week and Anti-Hype Review
Batch 11 Multi-Target Dataset and Metric Validation Program
UCI Glass Dataset Audit with csvkit Tool Execution
Batch 7 Banknote Metric Stress Validator

candidates.whyNow: Batch 10 and Batch 11 narrowed most custom tools to support or packaging roles.
candidates.expectedScientificValue: Prevents broad product promotion without reuse/failure evidence.
candidates.falsifiability: A tool is downgraded if simple baselines produce the same finding.
candidates.executionFeasibility: Uses existing public tool-decision corpus artifacts.
candidates.safetyScope: safe computational research only; no medical advice, wet-lab protocol, exploit, malware, private data, or legal patentability claim

candidates.title: Validate low-risk controls for protocol-first benchmark claims

sourceEvidence.title: UCI Wine Quality Public Dataset-Quality Audit
UCI Letter Recognition Vowel Benchmark Negative Challenge
Batch 7 Wine Quality Schema Provenance Tool

candidates.whyNow: Protocol-risk science needs controls to avoid cherry-picking high-risk targets.
candidates.expectedScientificValue: Tests whether protocol-first claims are scoped rather than overgeneralized.
candidates.falsifiability: If low-risk controls show large unexplained deltas, current scope claims must be revised.
candidates.executionFeasibility: Uses current benchmark execution harness and public datasets.
candidates.safetyScope: safe computational research only; no medical advice, wet-lab protocol, exploit, malware, private data, or legal patentability claim

candidates.title: Scientific dataset reliability audit with protocol extraction

candidates.whyNow: Batch 12 promoted scientific dataset reliability but narrowed schema discovery to packaging-only.
candidates.expectedScientificValue: Separates evidence packaging from true reliability findings.
candidates.falsifiability: Claims fail if ordinary pandas/sklearn checks explain all findings.
candidates.executionFeasibility: Uses public tabular data and existing schema packaging.

Batch 24 Protocol-Card Kill Week and Next Frontier

candidates.title: Time-series anomaly negative-control benchmark

sourceEvidence.title: NAB Time-Series Anomaly Benchmark Challenge
UCI Bike Sharing Time-Series Holdout Benchmark
UCI Air Quality Time-Series Dataset Audit
Do provenance-aware anomaly scoring methods reduce false positives in public-weather-proxy energy datasets compared with simple threshold baselines?

candidates.whyNow: Earlier dataset audits produced useful negatives but this domain has less program continuity.
candidates.expectedScientificValue: Tests whether anomaly claims survive simple negative controls.
candidates.falsifiability: Method claims fail if shuffled or naive seasonal baselines dominate.
candidates.executionFeasibility: Feasible but target selection needs more public protocol evidence.
candidates.safetyScope: safe computational research only; no medical advice, wet-lab protocol, exploit, malware, private data, or legal patentability claim
